questions.question: What is the primary purpose of an abstract class in Object-Oriented Programming?
options: A] To create a new instance of a class
B] To provide a blueprint for other classes to follow
C] To inherit properties and methods from another class
D] To define a constructor
questions.explanation: An abstract class provides a blueprint for other classes to follow and can have both abstract and concrete methods.

questions.question: What is the difference between a method and a function in Object-Oriented Programming?
options: A] A method is static, while a function is instance
B] A method is instance, while a function is static
C] A method is used for data encapsulation, while a function is used for data abstraction
D] A method is used for data abstraction, while a function is used for data encapsulation
questions.explanation: In Object-Oriented Programming, a method is a part of a class that is used to perform a specific action, while a function is a generic term used in other programming languages.

questions.question: What is polymorphism in Object-Oriented Programming?
options: A] The ability of an object to take on multiple forms
B] The ability of a class to be inherited by multiple subclasses
C] The ability of a method to be called with different parameters
D] The ability of an object to inherit properties and methods from multiple classes
questions.explanation: Polymorphism is the ability of an object to take on multiple forms, depending on the context in which it is used.

questions.question: What is encapsulation in Object-Oriented Programming?
options: A] The process of hiding the implementation details of an object from the outside world
B] The process of exposing the implementation details of an object to the outside world
C] The process of creating a new instance of a class
D] The process of defining a constructor
questions.explanation: Encapsulation is the process of hiding the implementation details of an object from the outside world and only exposing the necessary information through public methods.

questions.question: What is the difference between inheritance and polymorphism in Object-Oriented Programming?
options: A] Inheritance is the ability of a class to inherit properties and methods from another class, while polymorphism is the ability of an object to take on multiple forms
B] Inheritance is the ability of an object to inherit properties and methods from another class, while polymorphism is the ability of a class to be inherited by multiple subclasses
C] Inheritance is the ability of a class to be inherited by multiple subclasses, while polymorphism is the ability of an object to take on multiple forms
D] Inheritance is the ability of a class to be inherited by multiple subclasses, while polymorphism is the ability of an object to inherit properties and methods from multiple classes
questions.explanation: Inheritance is the process of one class inheriting properties and methods from another class, while polymorphism is the ability of an object to take on multiple forms, depending on the context in which it is used.
